repo_prior_art.cite: eid_data_fusion_k_source: its global overlap-hedge proposal does not state this label-restricted parity-model compatibility lemma.

published_prior_art.cite: LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic, §6 and Appendix B.3 already adapt parity witnesses to context-selected models.

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_data_fusion_k_source: no two-block context-severing certificate or context-coupling contrast is stated there.

published_prior_art.cite: LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic proves SS-ID soundness and partial nonidentification; ShpitserPearl2006Joint supplies the parity-hedge template, but neither states this proposed local criterion.
ShpitserPearl2006Joint: rooted-forest hedges and binary parity witnesses establish the ordinary nonidentification construction.

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_data_fusion_k_source: it does not characterize vanishing of a context-indexed coupling contrast in a binary parity family.

published_prior_art.cite: LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic leaves the remaining SS-ID failure case outside its partial-completeness theorem; no exact context-severing characterization was verified.

structure_flags.label: thm:context-marginal-sufficiency
structure_flags.one_line: The central open kernel is presented as a theorem while the only OEQ is secondary; relabel the unproved certificate as a conjecture or make the OEQ the headline.

novelty_flags.label: prop:ordinary-hedge-reduction
novelty_flags.one_line: The asserted ordinary rooted-forest parity witness is already supplied by ShpitserPearl2006Joint and is only a sanity reduction, not a novel result.

novelty_flags.label: thm:context-marginal-sufficiency / LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic
novelty_flags.one_line: Although the promise table calls Theorem 1 a strict tightening of SS-ID, no statement links its two-block object to a specified SS-ID Algorithm-2 failure condition or proves a comparator-facing frontier.

soundness_flags.label: def:parity-cs-scm / lem:parity-construction-compatibility
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed equations assign both S~pi and X_S by the XOR recursion, apply XOR to arbitrary finite state spaces, and omit the selector serious-value semantics required for a valid CS-SCM.

soundness_flags.label: thm:context-marginal-sufficiency
soundness_flags.one_line: Its conditions allow a block-to-root path to pass through A; after do(A=a) that path is cut, so one parity coordinate can never reach Y and Q_kappa need not vary with kappa despite both stated context-connectivity conditions.

soundness_flags.label: prop:ordinary-hedge-reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: With U empty the theorem supplies no equality for the unselected law P_empty, whereas the ordinary hedge witness requires equality of the observed law; the stated construction therefore does not reduce to that witness.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define a genuine finite binary CS-SCM: restrict XOR equations to V minus S, encode each selector's serious indicator and assigned value, and prove its induced label restriction is rho_u.
Add a post-intervention target-exposure condition ensuring both parity coordinates reach an ancestor of Y through paths avoiding A and S, then prove target separation on a worked labelled ADMG.
Replace the U-empty reduction with an explicit supplied empty context P_empty and prove equality there, or demote the proposition to an informal sanity check.

State and prove the exact implication from the repaired certificate to the specific uncovered SS-ID Algorithm-2 terminal failure, then formulate a necessary-and-sufficient frontier against that published branch.

recommended_next_step: Return to Stage -1 for a semantic rebuild of the CS-SCM construction and a repaired target-separation theorem before re-running novelty review.
